columns.description: Unique subject identifier for each individual in the study
Age of the subject in years
Body weight of the subject in kg
Sex of the subject; coded as 0 = Male, 1 = Female
Creatinine clearance, a measure of renal function in mL/min
Baseline serum creatinine concentration in mg/dL at treatment start
Diabetes diagnosis flag: 0 = no diabetes, 1 = diabetes
Hypertension diagnosis flag: 0 = no hypertension, 1 = hypertension
Number of prior nephrotoxic antibiotic therapy courses
Individual random effect (ETA) on clearance; deviation from typical value on log scale
Individual random effect (ETA) on central volume; deviation from typical value on log scale
Individual random effect (ETA) on intercompartmental clearance; deviation from typical value on log scale
Individual random effect (ETA) on peripheral volume; deviation from typical value on log scale
Area under the concentration-time curve over 24 hours (mg*h/L)
Predicted or estimated trough concentration (minimum concentration, mg/L)
Cumulative exposure over treatment course (sum of AUC values, mg*h/L)
Maximum concentration in the central compartment (mg/L)
Maximum concentration in the peripheral compartment (mg/L)
Binary nephrotoxicity outcome: 0 = no nephrotoxicity, 1 = nephrotoxicity
Model-derived nephrotoxicity risk probability score on 0-1 scale
Maximum increase in serum creatinine from baseline during follow-up (mg/dL)
Mean absolute individual weighted residuals summarizing fit quality
Number of concentration observations available for the subject
